sutta: anata pindika the householder visited the exalted 1 and saluting him sat down at 1 side and so seated the exalted 1 spoke to him and said there are these 5 things householder which are welcome sought after lovely but hard to get in the world what 5 long life householder is welcome sought after lovely but hard to get in the world beauty is welcome sought after lovely but hard to get in the world happiness honor the heaven worlds are welcome sought after lovely by heart to get in the world such householder are the 5 things which are welcome sought after lovely but hard to get in the world [Music] i'll just stop for a while to commend this is 1 of the uh very important sutas very very important concerning gamma the buddha said these 5 things aren't that hard to get nice long life everybody wants almost everybody wants long life and beauty beauty something that comes when a person is born with that physical body the third 1 is happiness the fourth 1 is honor having a good name a good reputation being well known and the fifth 1 is good rebirth these things are hard to get and everybody wants them now the buddha continued now these 5 things householder are not to be got either by vows or prayers i declare for if they were why should anyone languish here to bring about long life householder it is of no use for an aryan disciple yearning for long life either to pray for it or to think much of it the way that leads to long life must be walked by the aryan disciple and when the way is walked by him it leads to the winning of long life and he becomes a winner both of heavenly life and human life so too of beauty happiness honor householder to bring about life in the heaven worlds it is of no use for an aryan disciple yearning for heaven either to pray for it or to think much of it the steps that lead to heaven must be stepped by the aryan disciple and when those steps are stepped by him they lead to the winning of heaven and he becomes a winner of the heaven worlds that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so here the buddha is said it is saying that these things that we won cannot be caught by vows or prayers only because if they could be got by vows and prayers why should there be still so much suffering in the world and we know that all the buddhas and buddhism countless buddhas and bodhisattvas and holy men have come and is still the same the 3 woeful planes had the 3 woeful parts the hell world the ghost realm and the animal world all full with beings and they will continue to be full with beings and they will never be empty because the buddha says samsara will never end and time will never end and because time has no beginning and no end so samsara will never end and buddha said the only way here to get these things that we want is to walk the path that brings us to what we want for example if we want long life the buddha said in the suta on the kama that uh in 1 of the subtles about the buddha said long life is to be god by practicing the precept against killing that means if we don't kill other living beings that is the way to get long life because by allowing other beings to have long life we will also get long line and the way to get beauty here is not to have a bad temper not to get angry and salt etc because every time we get angry and show our temper we become fierce and when we become fierce it shows in our face and i keep on doing that and it becomes a habit and the face becomes fierce and so is not a lovely face and not a beautiful face whereas another person with a very good um natured person and doesn't get stirred up easily and whatever words you throw at him he takes it calmly with a sweet smile and so becomes a very sweet person and that makes for beauty and happiness there are many things to do to get happiness basically it is to live a skillful life love you know to do wholesome action wholesome things instead of unskillful or unwholesome actions and wholesome actions actions that benefit other beings and benefit us and holds them or evil actions are those actions that harm living beings other beings and harm us and the way to get honor as we've heard before in 1 of the sutas is not to be jealous of other people not to have a small heart not to be selfish and in that respect and and if that is so then we are not spiteful then we will have honor and heaven worlds are also to be gotten by skillful living living alive according to dhamma in accordance with dharma that will bring us to the heaven worlds the buddha said in the dharmapada that striving should be done by yourselves buddhas only show the way so the buddha helps us indirectly by showing us the path and we have to walk the path ourselves we have to choose which way to work whether walk the good way or walk the bad way and there's some other suta where the buddha made it quite clear that it's not possible for a buddha to uh make a vow to help us or to pray for us or to help us get our samsara if it were possible the buddha would have said so in the in these sutas but whereas the buddha said very clearly like in this that we have to work our own karma to to get the things we want and for that that's why we have to understand the principle of karma and b parker um if we do the right things for example if we don't kill long life will come about naturally we don't have to wish for it and for example uh also a lot of people like to be wealthy the way to be wealthy is to practice charity because when we practice charity we help others and in uh even in this life itself or in a future life there will be many people who want to help you so that when whether you are doing business you open a shop or whatever you do there will be many people who want to help you and so it is not difficult to become rich the the most important thing of all all that we want to wish for in the world is that is the wish to end our suffering and to end our suffering we have to take make the effort to walk the spiritual path if we don't make the effort to walk the spiritual path we will never get out of samsara because the buddha has said that the samsara will never end and so we have to learn the suttas understand the sutas the dhamma and we [Music] practice according to the dhamma um and we can see for ourselves even in this life when a person is about to pass away if he has a good understanding of the dhamma he has led a skillful life there will be no fear when death comes he'll be he'll take death very calmly some some people even very happily yeah because he knows he's going to a good rebirth now the nexuta is
